rationale.root_cause: At the FLAW site (memcpy at line ~42), it copies sizeof(*structCharVoid) bytes into charFirst[16], overflowing the heap buffer and overwriting voidSecond/voidThird. This corrupts the pointer and leads to a crash when it is later used.
rationale.local_fix: Replace the memcpy length with sizeof(structCharVoid->charFirst) so only the field's size is copied. This prevents overwriting adjacent struct members.
rationale.global_fix: Use a bounded copy helper or typed buffer wrapper (e.g., memcpy_s or a SAFE_MEMCPY macro with sizeof checks/_Static_assert) to ensure destination field size is enforced. Such an abstraction prevents confusing struct size with field size in future copies.
